pi_items.source: NetworkOptix Nx Witness documentation confirms port 7001 is the default REST API endpoint. Verified in task_utils.sh line 4, setup_nx_witness.sh line 9, and multiple setup scripts.

Nx Witness documentation confirms 'admin' is the default admin username. Password 'Admin1234!' is set during system initialization in setup_nx_witness.sh line 78 via the /rest/v1/system/setup API. Credentials are also referenced in task_utils.sh line 5 and setup_task.sh line 17.

FPS value of 7 is hardcoded in setup_task.sh lines 58 and 84 as the unique configuration signature for the faulty camera. Verified in verifier.py line 42 which checks for EXPECTED_FPS = 7. Nx Witness API documentation confirms FPS values up to 30 are supported, making 7 a valid choice.

Quality value 'low' is hardcoded in setup_task.sh lines 57 and 84 as part of the unique configuration signature. Verified in verifier.py line 42 which checks for EXPECTED_QUALITY = 'low'. Nx Witness documentation confirms valid quality options include Lowest, Low, Medium, High, and Best.

Path is specified in export_result.sh line 9 and matches the metadata in task.json line 21. The /home/ga directory is the home directory of the 'ga' user who runs the task environment. Path is writable by the ga user for the maintenance report output.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses NetworkOptix Nx Witness VMS running on localhost at port 7001 with admin credentials (admin/Admin1234!). The specific recording schedule to be migrated consists of FPS=7 and quality='low', which serve as unique identifiers for detecting the correct configuration. The maintenance report must be written to /home/ga/maintenance_report.json as valid JSON. All technical specifications match official Nx Witness API documentation and are correctly implemented in the task setup scripts.
